name: Finland

colors.symbolism: the snow that covers Finland in winter
the country's thousands of lakes and the sky

description: A white field with a blue Nordic cross; the vertical arm is shifted toward the hoist.
history: Adopted on 29 May 1918, soon after Finland's declaration of independence from Russia (6 December 1917). The blue-and-white scheme had been promoted as Finnish national colours since the mid-19th century — notably by the poet Zacharias Topelius — and several rival designs (including red-and-yellow versions echoing the Finnish coat of arms) were considered before the present Nordic cross was chosen. The current shades and exact proportions were laid down in the Flag Act of 26 May 1978.

features.symbolism: the off-centre Nordic cross expresses Christian heritage and kinship with the other Nordic nations; the white-and-blue colour scheme evokes the snow-covered land and the country's many lakes and skies
